title: rubus × collium f. mediocris
intro: Rubus × collium f. mediocris, also known as wild raspberry, is a hybrid plant that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae) and is a cross between the red raspberry (Rubus idaeus) and the black raspberry (Rubus occidentalis). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its high content of antioxidants, anthocyanins, and ellagic acid, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and anticancer properties. The health benefits of Rubus × collium f. mediocris include its ability to protect against cell damage, reduce inflammation, and support immune function, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and capsules, which can be used to treat conditions such as digestive issues, skin problems, and cardiovascular disease.
